title: Samsung Healthcare HM70A Ultrasound System - Probe Not Recognized or Port Damage
description: Troubleshooting when the HM70A does not recognize a probe or shows intermittent connection due to probe, cable, or port-related issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the ultrasound system from active patient use if imaging is required. Move the patient to a functioning system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Connect the probe and power on the system. Observe for “Probe not recognized” message, no image display, or intermittent connection when moving cable.

steps.title: Check Probe Connection (Most Common Cause)
steps.instructions: Disconnect and firmly reconnect the probe to the port. Ensure connector is fully seated and locking mechanism (if present) is engaged.

steps.title: Inspect Probe Connector Pins
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the probe connector for bent or missing pins, contamination (gel, dust, debris), or corrosion.

steps.title: Inspect System Port for Damage
steps.instructions: Use a flashlight to inspect the port for bent or recessed pins, debris, or physical damage.

steps.title: Clean Connector and Port (If Needed)
steps.instructions: Use approved cleaning methods if contamination is present. Do not insert anything that could damage pins.

steps.title: Test with a Known-Good Probe
steps.instructions: Connect a different compatible probe to determine if the issue is probe-related or system-related.

steps.title: Test the Suspect Probe on Another System (If Available)
steps.instructions: Connect the original probe to another compatible ultrasound system to confirm whether the probe is defective.

steps.title: Check for Intermittent Connection
steps.instructions: Gently move the probe cable near the connector while observing system response.

steps.title: Reboot the System
steps.instructions: Power the system off, wait 15–30 seconds, and restart to clear temporary communication faults.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound probe not recognized on Samsung HM70A; no image displayed.
CCR.Cause: Damaged probe connector pins causing loss of communication with system.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defective probe from service and replaced with known-good probe; confirmed normal operation.

helpfulDetails: Presence of bent pins or debris

title: Samsung Healthcare HM70A Ultrasound System - Image Dropout or Quality Degradation
description: Troubleshooting intermittent or poor ultrasound image quality caused by probe, connection, or system setting issues.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to another functioning ultrasound system before troubleshooting if imaging is critical.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the system and determine if the issue is complete or partial dropout, intermittent or constant, and if it affects specific probes or all probes.

steps.title: Check Probe Connection
steps.instructions: Reconnect the probe securely and inspect for loose connection, bent pins, or debris in the port.

steps.title: Test with a Known Good Probe
steps.instructions: Use a different probe to determine if the issue is probe-related or system-related.

steps.title: Inspect the Probe Cable
steps.instructions: Check for physical damage and observe for image flickering when moving the cable.

steps.title: Check Imaging Settings
steps.instructions: Verify gain, depth, and frequency settings and reset to default presets if needed.

steps.title: Check for Overheating
steps.instructions: Assess system temperature and fan operation, especially after prolonged use.

steps.title: Inspect Probe Port
steps.instructions: Look for bent pins, loose connectors, or physical damage in the probe port.

steps.title: Perform a System Reboot
steps.instructions: Restart the system to clear temporary software or processing issues.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound image intermittently dropping out during use on Samsung HM70A.
CCR.Cause: Faulty linear probe cable causing intermittent signal loss when manipulated.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced defective probe with known good unit; verified normal image quality restored.

helpfulDetails: Alarm or error behavior during imaging
Environmental factors such as heat or extended use

title: Samsung Healthcare HM70A Ultrasound System - System Fails to Boot or Crashes
description: Troubleshooting boot failures, crashes, or restart issues caused by power, accessories, or system faults.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the system from clinical use if it is unreliable. Move the patient to another ultrasound system before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify Power Source and AC Adapter
steps.instructions: Confirm the system is plugged into a known-good outlet. Check the AC adapter for secure connection, indicator lights, and damage.

steps.title: Check Battery Status (If Equipped)
steps.instructions: Disconnect AC power and attempt to power on using battery only. Then reconnect AC and observe charging indicators.

steps.title: Perform a Full Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Shut down the system completely. Disconnect AC power and remove battery if accessible. Hold the power button for 10–15 seconds, then reconnect power and restart.

steps.title: Disconnect All External Accessories
steps.instructions: Remove all probes and USB/network devices and attempt to boot the system with no peripherals connected.

steps.title: Test with Known-Good Probe (After Boot)
steps.instructions: If the system boots without accessories, reconnect one known-good probe to verify stability.

steps.title: Observe Boot Behavior and Error Messages
steps.instructions: Identify whether the system shows no display, freezes on logo, reboots repeatedly, or displays error messages.

steps.title: Check for Overheating or Environmental Issues
steps.instructions: Ensure vents are not blocked and check for excessive heat, dust buildup, or abnormal fan noise.

steps.title: Attempt Safe Restart / Software Recovery (If Available)
steps.instructions: Use system recovery or safe startup mode per facility protocol if accessible.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound system would not boot and intermittently restarted during use.
CCR.Cause: System failed to boot with accessories connected; issue isolated after removing faulty probe.
CCR.Resolution: Removed defective probe from service; system booted normally and returned to operation.

helpfulDetails: Presence of heat, noise, or fan issues
Final system status (working or sent for repair)

title: Samsung Healthcare HM70A Ultrasound System - DICOM or Network Export Failure
description: Failure to send images to PACS, DICOM transfer errors, network disconnects, or studies not appearing in the archive.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Do not delay patient care while troubleshooting. Use an alternate ultrasound system if image export is required immediately.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Attempt to send a test image or study to PACS. Observe error messages, send failed or timeout messages, and lack of confirmation.

steps.title: Check Network Cable Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect Ethernet cable at system and wall port. Ensure it is fully seated and check for link/activity lights.

steps.title: Verify Network Connectivity
steps.instructions: Check network settings for valid IP address and connectivity. Ping PACS server if tools are available.

steps.title: Confirm DICOM Configuration Settings
steps.instructions: Verify AE Title, PACS IP address, port, and device name match hospital configuration.

steps.title: Perform a Test Send
steps.instructions: Send a test study and confirm it appears in PACS.

steps.title: Check for Network Restrictions
steps.instructions: Verify with IT there are no VLAN, firewall, or port restrictions affecting communication.

steps.title: Restart the System
steps.instructions: Power down completely, wait 30 seconds, and restart the system.

steps.title: Try Alternate Network Port
steps.instructions: Connect to a known working network port to rule out infrastructure issues.

CCR.Complaint: Ultrasound unable to send studies to PACS; export failing.
CCR.Cause: Incorrect DICOM AE Title configured, preventing communication with PACS server.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected AE Title and verified successful test transmission to PACS.

helpfulDetails: DICOM settings reviewed (AE Title, IP, port)

title: Samsung Healthcare HM70A Ultrasound System - Touchscreen or Control Panel Unresponsive
description: Troubleshooting an unresponsive touchscreen or control panel, including power, lock settings, peripheral issues, and external causes affecting input response.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the system from active patient use before troubleshooting. Move the patient to another ultrasound system if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Issue
steps.instructions: Power on the system and confirm the touchscreen or control panel is unresponsive. Determine if the entire screen or only certain areas are affected and whether buttons, trackball, or keyboard are also affected.

steps.title: Check for Screen Lock or Freeze Condition
steps.instructions: Verify the system is not in a locked or frozen state. Look for lock icons or inactive UI indicators and attempt to use physical freeze or unlock controls.

steps.title: Perform a System Reboot
steps.instructions: Power down the system completely, wait 15–30 seconds, then restart.

steps.title: Inspect for External Obstructions or Contamination
steps.instructions: Check for gel residue, dirt, debris, or moisture on the touchscreen and control panel. Clean using approved disinfectant wipes.

steps.title: Check Connected Accessories and Peripherals
steps.instructions: Disconnect external devices such as USB devices, keyboards, mice, or network cables to rule out interference.

steps.title: Test Alternate Input Methods
steps.instructions: Attempt to use trackball, physical buttons, or keyboard to determine if some inputs still function.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Check for cracked screen, loose or damaged control panel, or signs of impact or wear.

steps.title: Check for System Performance Issues
steps.instructions: Observe if the system is slow, freezing, or lagging prior to becoming unresponsive.

CCR.Complaint: Touchscreen and control panel not responding during ultrasound exam.
CCR.Cause: Touchscreen remained unresponsive after reboot and cleaning; likely internal digitizer or control interface failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed unit from service, labeled out of service, and sent for vendor repair.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Removed from service
